mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and rising capacity utilization, which translates directly into higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points from its trailing 3-month high signals accelerating labor market recovery, boosting household income expectations and aggregate equity demand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.2pp from its 3-month trailing high
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or below 2 percent annualized in more than 50 percent of trigger events during high unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) drive real interest rates deeply negative, forcing capital out of cash and money-market instruments into equities and producing positive reflation-driven returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL rising month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and rising CPIAUCSL fail to generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) generates positive S&P 500 returns in the early post-recession recovery phase because pent-up consumer demand drives revenue surprises and supports multiple expansion for consumer-facing firms.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS surprises coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of instances during early recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal a durable recovery in residential construction and durable goods demand, lifting broad equity indices through construction sector earnings and associated multiplier effects.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.0% imposes restrictive borrowing costs on corporations and consumers, reduces present values of future cash flows, and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Episodes with FEDFUNDS above 2.0% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm a genuine labor market recovery rather than a measurement artifact, driving sustained equity appreciation through consumption growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive PAYEMS changes and negative UNRATE changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) alongside falling retail sales (RSAFS) signals involuntary inventory accumulation and impending margin compression, which causes negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: The combination of INDPRO increasing and RSAFS decreasing produces positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of joint occurrences over a 2-year rolling window.
